classes.materials: Ruler, tape measure, design spec worksheet, ergonomics reference card, projector

classes.title: Model in Tinkercad – Structural Design
classes.description: Students model the stand: base plate (wide for stability), back support (angled at 65–70°), phone cradle (two prongs with phone-width gap). Use constraints from design spec. Apply shell technique: outer box minus inner hole = thin walls.
classes.materials: Computers with Tinkercad, parametric design checklist, virtual ruler on Tinkercad

classes.title: Structural Analysis & Iteration
classes.description: Test virtual design: will it tip over? (Check centre of gravity by comparing base footprint to back weight.) Does the cradle fit a typical phone (15cm × 8cm)? Revise minimum 2 structural elements based on analysis. Export STL. Present with design rationale.
classes.materials: Tinkercad, phone dimensions reference, stability analysis worksheet, STL export

projects.project:
Project 8 – PIR Security Alarm System
(Dual PIR + Door Sensor + Buzzer)

classes.title: Advanced Security Systems Architecture
classes.description: Map a real security system: PIR zone 1, zone 2, door sensor, siren, control panel. Discuss: false alarm problem. Introduce: dual-zone verification (both PIR + door sensor must trigger = real intrusion). Students design the dual-zone logic diagram.
classes.materials: Projector, security system architecture diagram, false alarm statistics article

classes.title: Wire Dual-Zone PIR + Door Sensor
classes.description: Wire 2 PIR sensors (Zone A and Zone B) + reed switch (door sensor). Logic: If (Zone_A OR Zone_B) AND door_open → alarm. Wire piezo buzzer, red LED, and green (safe) LED. Code multi-zone logic. Test: trigger one zone alone (no alarm), trigger with door (alarm).
classes.materials: 2 PIR sensors, reed switch + magnet, piezo buzzer, LEDs, Arduino, breadboard, wires

classes.title: Add Control Panel & Demo Full Alarm Cycle
classes.description: Add a keypad to arm/disarm with a 3-digit code. Build a cardboard control panel with zone indicator LEDs, armed/disarmed LED, and buzzer. Print ARMED or DISARMED on LCD. Demonstrate the full armed → triggered → disarmed cycle to the class.
classes.materials: 4×4 keypad, 16×2 LCD, cardboard control panel, completed circuit

projects.project:
Project 9 – Energy Consumption Monitor
(Current Sensor + Power Dashboard)

classes.title: Electricity Bills & Energy Monitoring
classes.description: Show a real electricity bill. Calculate: units used × rate = cost. 1 unit = 1 kWh. A 60W bulb for 1 hour = 0.06 units. Introduce ACS712 current sensor: Hall-effect chip measures AC or DC current. Demo: measure current flowing through a known resistor.
classes.materials: Electricity bill printout, ACS712 module, Arduino, known resistor, multimeter, calculation worksheet

classes.title: Wire Current Sensor & Calculate Power
classes.description: Wire ACS712 → Arduino A0. Calibrate: measure voltage at A0 with no load → zero-current baseline (~2.5V). Code: current = (analogRead(A0)/1023.0 * 5.0 − 2.5) / sensitivity. Calculate: power = voltage × current. Display on Serial Monitor.
classes.materials: ACS712 30A module, Arduino, 5V DC load (fan/LED strip), laptop, power calculation worksheet

classes.title: Build the Power Dashboard on LCD
classes.description: Display on 20×4 LCD: V: 5.0V  A: 0.42A on line 1; W: 2.1W on line 2; Total kWh on line 3; Cost in rupees on line 4. Track cumulative energy using time × power integration in code. Build in a cardboard smart meter housing.
classes.materials: 20×4 LCD, I2C backpack, completed current sensor circuit, cardboard housing, rupee rate input variable

projects.project:
Project 10 – Startup Innovation Project
(Design Thinking + Pitch + Prototype)

classes.title: Entrepreneur Mindset – Problem to Product
classes.description: Case studies: Ola (taxi problem), Zomato (food delivery problem), BYJU'S (education access problem). Students brainstorm 5 problems in school/home/community. Use SCAMPER technique to innovate. Select best idea and write a 1-sentence value proposition.
classes.materials: Projector, startup case study cards, SCAMPER worksheet, sticky notes

classes.title: Build the MVP (Minimum Viable Prototype)
classes.description: Students build the simplest working version of their idea. It must: (1) work (even partially); (2) address the identified problem; (3) be demonstrable. Combine any Grade 6 skills: Python, Arduino, sensors, 3D design. Creativity encouraged.
classes.materials: Full mixed STEM kit, Python computers, Tinkercad access, 35-minute build sprint, build log

classes.title: Startup Pitch Day – Demo & Investor Q&A
classes.description: 5-minute pitch to panel: Problem (1 min), Solution demo (2 min), Business model idea (1 min), Ask (1 min). Panel asks 2 questions. Class votes: Which startup would you invest in? Awards: Best Pitch, Most Innovative, Best Demo.
classes.materials: Completed prototype, pitch presentation (5 slides or poster), voting system, awards

projects.project:
Project 1 – Voice-Controlled Robot
(Python + Speech Recognition + pySerial)

classes.title: Speech Recognition – How Machines Understand Voice
classes.description: Discuss: how does Siri/Alexa convert speech to text? Feature extraction (MFCC) → phoneme matching → word prediction. Introduce Python SpeechRecognition library: import speech_recognition as sr; recognizer.listen() → recognize_google(). Run demo: speak command → text appears.
classes.materials: Computers with Python, SpeechRecognition library (pip install), microphone, projector

classes.title: Map Voice Commands to Robot Actions
classes.description: Code command dictionary: forward → F, back → B, left → L, right → R, stop → S. Continuous loop: listen → recognise → look up in dict → send character via pyserial to Arduino COM port. Arduino receives character and drives motors accordingly.
classes.materials: Python (pyserial + SpeechRecognition), Arduino with motor driver, robot chassis, USB cable

classes.title: Build Robot & Test Voice Commands
classes.description: Assemble robot (reuse Bluetooth chassis if available). Upload motor driver Arduino code. Run Python on laptop. Test commands: forward, stop, left, right. Add: spin (rotate 360°), dance (random moves for 5s). Evaluate accuracy: how many commands recognised correctly in 20 tries?
classes.materials: Assembled robot, Arduino motor code, Python laptop, obstacle course, USB cable, mic, accuracy log

projects.project:
Project 2 – Face Recognition Door System
(OpenCV + Python + Servo Lock)

classes.title: Computer Vision Fundamentals – How Machines See
classes.description: Introduce: pixels, RGB, grayscale. Haar cascade face detection in OpenCV. Demo: cv2.VideoCapture(0) + face_cascade.detectMultiScale() → real-time face detection with rectangles drawn. Students run the demo and adjust scale factor and minNeighbors parameters.
classes.materials: Computers with OpenCV (cv2) installed, webcam, projector, face detection worksheet

classes.title: Train a Face Recogniser – LBPH Algorithm
classes.description: Capture 20 face images of 3 students (with different expressions/angles). Train LBPH recogniser: recogniser.train(images, labels). Test on new photo → predict name with confidence score. Discuss: why does confidence drop with glasses or different lighting?
classes.materials: Computers, webcam, OpenCV with cv2.face module, image capture script, training script

classes.title: Integrate with Arduino Servo Door Lock
classes.description: When recognised face has confidence > 70%: send U (unlock) to Arduino via serial → servo rotates to 90° (unlocked) → 5s delay → servo back to 0° (locked). Unknown face → LED stays red. Build a cardboard door model with servo-based latch.
classes.materials: Arduino with servo, cardboard door model, USB serial, Python recognition code, servo latch mechanism

projects.project:
Project 3 – Adaptive Traffic Management System
(Multi-Sensor + Adaptive Timing)

classes.title: Adaptive Traffic Systems – How Smart Cities Work
classes.description: Compare: fixed-timing lights vs. adaptive systems. Adaptive: sensors measure vehicle queue length → algorithm allocates green time proportionally. Students map an intersection with 4 arms. Design sensor placement and flow logic diagram.
classes.materials: Projector, ATMS article, intersection design worksheet, traffic flow data

classes.title: Wire Multi-LED + IR Queue Sensors
classes.description: 4-arm model on A1 cardboard. Each arm: IR sensor (detects car presence), 3 LEDs (R/Y/G). Wire to Arduino Mega. Code initial fixed-timing sequence (30s green/5s yellow per arm cyclic). Test with toy cars placed in each arm.
classes.materials: Arduino Mega, 4 IR sensors, 12 LEDs (3R/3Y/3G), 12 resistors, A1 cardboard, toy cars

classes.title: Implement Adaptive Timing Algorithm
classes.description: Replace fixed timing: read all 4 IR sensors → assign green time proportional to detected density: arm with most cars detected → gets 40s; arm with no cars → gets 10s. Code adaptive cycle. Test: heavy traffic arm gets longer green. Observe fairness vs. efficiency trade-off.
classes.materials: Arduino Mega with adaptive code, 4-arm traffic model, toy cars for testing, timing log sheet

classes.title: Simulation & Optimisation – Reduce Average Wait
classes.description: Record: with fixed timing, how long does 1 car wait on average? With adaptive timing, repeat. Calculate average wait reduction %. Students tweak min/max green times and record effects. Present: Our adaptive system reduces average wait by __% in this test.
classes.materials: Traffic model, stopwatch, wait time recording sheet, calculator, presentation template

projects.project:
Project 4 – Python Automated Attendance
(Face Recognition + CSV Logging)

classes.title: Attendance System Architecture – End-to-End Design
classes.description: Map the full system: webcam → OpenCV detect face → match to database → log to CSV with timestamp → generate daily report. Discuss: privacy concerns. What should be stored? What should not? Design data schema for the student records and attendance tables.
classes.materials: Projector, system architecture diagram, CSV schema design worksheet, privacy discussion cards

classes.title: Build the Face Recognition Engine
classes.description: Enrol 5 students: capture 20 images each. Train LBPH. Build a recognise() function that returns (name, confidence). Test accuracy on held-out photos. Set threshold: confidence > 60 = identified; else = Unknown. Print result with colour-coded terminal output.
classes.materials: Python, OpenCV, webcam, enrollment script, recognition accuracy test set, terminal

classes.title: Add CSV Logging & Daily Report
classes.description: Add attendance logging: if identified AND not already marked today → append row to attendance.csv: (name, date, time). Generate daily report: read CSV → count present per student → print summary. Export as formatted HTML report. Test with 5 students.
classes.materials: Python (csv, datetime, pandas), attendance logging script, HTML report template, test data

projects.project:
Project 5 – Robotic Arm with Python GUI
(Multi-Servo + pySerial Control)

classes.title: Robotic Arm Kinematics – Where Should the Hand Go?
classes.description: Introduce forward kinematics: given joint angles, where is the end effector? Calculate XY position for a 2-joint arm using trigonometry (L1×cos(θ1) + L2×cos(θ1+θ2)). Students manually calculate positions for 3 angle combinations and verify on a printed arm diagram.
classes.materials: Printed 2-joint arm diagram, angle worksheet, calculator, kinematics reference

classes.title: Build the Python Tkinter GUI Controller
classes.description: Build a Python tkinter GUI: 4 sliders (0–180°) for 4 servo joints. Each slider change → send serial command S1:090 (Servo 1, 90°) to Arduino. Arduino parses command and sets servo angle. Students customise slider labels and add colour-coded joint indicators.
classes.materials: Python with tkinter, Arduino with servo driver, pySerial, servo arm (from Gr5 or new build)

classes.title: Save & Replay Arm Sequences
classes.description: Add Record and Play buttons to GUI. Record: save all slider values every 0.5s to a list. Play: replay values in sequence, sending to Arduino. Students record: (1) pick up object, (2) move to bin, (3) release. Playback loops the task automatically.
classes.materials: Python GUI with record/play, Arduino servo arm, small objects (erasers), target zone

projects.project:
Project 6 – Data Visualisation Dashboard
(Pandas + Matplotlib + Real Data)

classes.title: Data Science Pipeline – Import, Clean, Analyse
classes.description: Introduce pandas: read a CSV of real data (Indian city weather data or electricity data). Explore: df.head(), df.info(), df.describe(). Find missing values: df.isnull().sum(). Drop or fill nulls. Filter: df[df['city']=='Mumbai']. Students explore one dataset independently.
classes.materials: Computers with pandas installed, sample CSV (weather or electricity), Jupyter or IDLE, analysis worksheet

classes.title: Visualise with Matplotlib & Seaborn
classes.description: Create 4 chart types: (1) Line chart: temperature over time; (2) Bar chart: city-wise comparison; (3) Scatter: temp vs. humidity; (4) Heatmap: correlation matrix. Teach: labels, titles, colour palettes, figure size. Students create all 4 charts.
classes.materials: Python with matplotlib + seaborn, dataset, chart creation worksheet, colour palette guide

classes.title: Build a Dashboard & Present Insights
classes.description: Combine 4 charts into one matplotlib figure (2×2 subplot layout). Add a title and data source footnote. Export as PNG. Students write 3 data insights: The data shows that ___. Present dashboard: I analysed ___ data. The most surprising finding was ___.
classes.materials: Python, subplot tutorial, dashboard template, insight writing worksheet, presentation space

projects.project:
Project 7 – Precision Gear Design
(CAD + Mechanical Engineering Principles)

classes.title: Gear Mechanics – Ratios, Torque & Speed
classes.description: Explain: gear ratio = teeth_output / teeth_input. If gear ratio = 2:1, speed halves and torque doubles. Show gear train animations. Demo: hand-turn a small gear → large gear turns slower with more force. Students calculate ratios for 3 gear combinations.
classes.materials: Physical gear set (demonstration), gear ratio animation, calculation worksheet, projector

classes.title: Design Gears in Tinkercad
classes.description: Use Tinkercad Gear Generator (Codeblocks module) or Gear Generator website + import STL. Design: a 20-tooth driver gear meshing with a 40-tooth driven gear (2:1 ratio). Correct: module (tooth size) must match for gears to mesh. Export as two separate STL files.
classes.materials: Computers with Tinkercad or Gear Generator website, gear design specification sheet

classes.title: Test Mesh & Design Gear Train Application
classes.description: If printed: test physical meshing. If not: test virtual mesh in Tinkercad. Students extend design: add a third idler gear. Design context: what device uses this gear train? (Clock, bicycle derailleur, conveyor belt.) Create a product card describing the application.
classes.materials: Tinkercad or printed gears, meshing test, product application card, presentation

projects.project:
Project 8 – Electronic Blind Stick
(Ultrasonic + Vibration Motor + Multi-Zone)

classes.title: Assistive Technology for Visual Impairment
classes.description: Guest speaker video: blind person navigating with a cane. Challenges: overhead obstacles, stairs, water. Research: electronic white cane technologies (Ultracane, WeWALK). Students map the sensing requirements: range, direction, feedback type (audio vs. haptic).
classes.materials: Projector, assistive tech videos, user needs mapping worksheet, haptic vs. audio comparison

classes.title: Wire Multi-Zone Distance Alert System
classes.description: Wire 3 ultrasonic sensors: front (main), low (step/water), upper (overhead). Wire 3 vibration motors (haptic) corresponding to each zone. Code: If front_dist < 100cm → vibrate proportional to closeness; If low_dist < 30cm → rapid vibrate; If overhead < 180cm → single pulse.
classes.materials: 3 HC-SR04, 3 vibration motors (coin type), MOSFETs for vibration motor drive, Arduino Mega, wires

classes.title: Build the Stick Housing & Test with Blindfold
classes.description: Mount sensors and motors in a PVC pipe stick (1 metre). Electronics in a handle housing. Run wire internally. Test: one student blindfolded holds the stick and navigates a room with obstacles. Count: how many obstacles detected vs. missed? Calculate detection rate.
classes.materials: PVC pipe (1m), handle housing, completed circuit, blindfold, obstacle course, navigation score sheet

projects.project:
Project 9 – Real-Time Energy Dashboard
(ACS712 + Python + Flask Web Dashboard)

classes.title: IoT Architecture – From Sensor to Dashboard
classes.description: Explain full IoT stack: Sensor → Microcontroller → Serial/Wi-Fi → Python → Database → Web Dashboard. Show a live Thingspeak or Grafana dashboard. Students map each layer for their energy monitoring system.
classes.materials: Projector, IoT architecture diagram, Thingspeak demo, latency worksheet

classes.title: Arduino Serial → Python Real-Time Read
classes.description: Arduino: read ACS712 current sensor, calculate power, print CSV line every second: time,voltage,current,power. Python: pyserial read loop, parse CSV, append to pandas DataFrame. Print live: Current: 0.42A, Power: 2.1W. Test accuracy vs. bench multimeter.
classes.materials: ACS712 module, Arduino, Python with pyserial + pandas, bench multimeter, load (fan/lamp)

classes.title: Build the Web Dashboard with Flask
classes.description: Use Flask + Chart.js: Python Flask serves a web page with a live updating line chart (power vs. time) using AJAX polling every 2 seconds. Students connect from another laptop's browser — see live power data. Add: daily kWh and cost display.
classes.materials: Python Flask, Chart.js (CDN), AJAX, completed Arduino data feed, second laptop for web view

projects.project:
Project 10 – AI for Social Good
(ML Model + Real Problem + Impact Presentation)

classes.title: AI Use Cases in Social Development
classes.description: Case studies: (1) Google Flood Forecasting in India → 7-day flood warnings; (2) AI for crop disease detection on smartphones; (3) Satellite AI for poaching detection in forests. Students choose a social problem domain: health, agriculture, education, or environment.
classes.materials: Projector, AI for social good case studies, domain selection worksheet

classes.title: Build a Proof-of-Concept AI Model
classes.description: Students use Teachable Machine (Google) or a Python sklearn classifier. Collect or use a provided dataset (50+ examples per class). Train classifier. Evaluate: accuracy, confusion matrix. Build a simple prediction interface (Tkinter form or Teachable Machine export).
classes.materials: Computers with Teachable Machine or sklearn, dataset (images/CSV), model evaluation worksheet

classes.title: Social Impact Presentation & Community Pitch
classes.description: Students present: (1) Problem and affected community; (2) AI solution demo; (3) Data behind the model (accuracy, limitations); (4) Ethical concerns (bias, privacy, consent); (5) Next steps to scale. Audience: students + teachers + invited community member.
classes.materials: Completed AI model, impact presentation (5–7 slides), demo laptop, evaluation rubric

projects.project:
Project 1 – Object Detection System
(OpenCV + Teachable Machine)

classes.title: Introduction to Computer Vision – How Machines See
classes.description: Demonstrate object detection using a phone camera + Google Lens. Explain the concept: images → pixels → features → labels. Show a pre-trained model detecting 5 objects in real-time. Students fill a 'Machine Perception Worksheet': label pixel grids, identify features (edges, corners, colour). Discuss real-world use: self-checkout, security cameras, medical imaging.
classes.materials: Projector, Google Lens demo, pixel grid worksheets, 5 sample objects

classes.title: Collect & Train – Build Your Own Image Classifier
classes.description: Students use Teachable Machine (Google). In groups: choose 3 object classes (e.g. pen, eraser, book). Capture 80+ photos per class using webcam. Train the model (observe training graphs). Test accuracy: hold up objects one by one. Record: true positives, false positives. Concept: what confuses the model?
classes.materials: Laptops/tablets with webcam, Teachable Machine (browser), classification result sheet

classes.title: Export & Embed – Use the Model in a Program
classes.description: Export model as TensorFlow.js (for web) or as a saved model. Load into a Python script using the tensorflow/keras library. Run predictions on 5 unseen test images. Print predicted class + confidence score. Students compare: model accuracy on train data vs. unseen test images. Discuss: overfitting, data diversity.
classes.materials: Python 3 with tensorflow, PIL, numpy; pre-downloaded test images (15 per group), accuracy comparison worksheet

projects.project:
Project 2 – Farming Robot (Line Follower + Soil Sensor + Spray)
(Agriculture Automation)

classes.title: Precision Agriculture – Why Robots Farm
classes.description: Video: drone sprayers in Indian farms, GPS-guided tractors, soil moisture sensors. Discussion: What are the costs of over-watering vs. under-watering crops? Students map a 'smart farm block diagram': sensors → controller → actuator. Define robot's job: follow path between crop rows, detect dry soil, spray water.
classes.materials: Projector, precision agriculture video, farm block diagram worksheet

classes.title: Build the Chassis & Line Following
classes.description: Assemble 2-wheel drive chassis with IR sensor array (3 sensors). Load line-following code: read 3 IR values, compute weighted error, drive motors. Test on a black-tape track (straight + curved). Tune motor speeds for smooth tracking. Measure: max speed without losing track.
classes.materials: 2WD robot kit, Arduino Uno, 3× IR sensors, L298N motor driver, black electrical tape track, battery

classes.title: Add Soil Sensor + Pump – Conditional Spraying
classes.description: Wire capacitive soil moisture sensor to Arduino A0. Connect 5V mini water pump via relay module. Code: IF soil_moisture < threshold → stop robot → run pump for 3 seconds → resume line following. Test on track with 2 dry zones (marked). Students tune moisture threshold values.
classes.materials: Capacitive soil moisture sensor, 5V mini pump, relay module, water tube, small tray, completed line-follower chassis

projects.project:
Project 3 – Autonomous Delivery Bot
(Path Planning + Obstacle Avoidance)

classes.title: Path Planning Algorithms – Breadth-First & A*
classes.description: On a printed 8×8 grid map (classroom layout), students manually run BFS: expand nodes in order, mark distances, trace shortest path. Compare with A* (use Manhattan distance as heuristic). Record: nodes explored, path length. Discuss: when is A* faster? Translate to robot: grid = floor tiles, path = motor commands.
classes.materials: Printed 8×8 grid maps (one per student), coloured pens (two colours for BFS vs A*), heuristic worksheet

classes.title: Build the Delivery Bot – Sensors + Encoders
classes.description: Wire ultrasonic sensor (front obstacle detection) and IR sensors (left, right wall detection) on a 2WD chassis. Add simple wheel encoders (slotted disk + IR sensor) to measure distance. Code: move forward 1 tile = encoder count threshold. Test: navigate a 4-tile straight, stop at wall.
classes.materials: 2WD chassis, Arduino Mega, HC-SR04 ultrasonic, 2× IR sensor, wheel encoder set, L298N motor driver

classes.title: Test the Full Delivery Mission
classes.description: Set up a mini 'warehouse': 3 delivery zones marked on a mat. Programme: start → scan for obstacle (ultrasonic) → navigate to zone 1 → signal arrival (buzzer + LED) → return. Run 5 deliveries, measure: success rate, average time, collisions. Each group reports results and suggests improvements.
classes.materials: Complete delivery bot, marked floor mat (3 zones), buzzer, 2× LEDs, stopwatch, mission result scorecard

projects.project:
Project 4 – Emotion Recognition App
(CNN + DeepFace / Teachable Machine)

classes.title: Emotion AI – How Computers Read Faces
classes.description: Demo DeepFace library on teacher's laptop — webcam detects emotions in real-time. Explain: Facial Action Coding System (FACS) → 43 muscle movements → 7 universal expressions. Students map face sketches: label muscles/expressions. Discuss ethical dimensions: Is this surveillance? What if it's wrong 30% of the time? Who owns facial data?
classes.materials: Projector, DeepFace demo laptop, face expression worksheet, ethical analysis card

classes.title: Python + DeepFace – Build the Emotion Reader
classes.description: Students install deepface and opencv-python. Code: open webcam, detect face with OpenCV (Haar cascade), pass crop to DeepFace.analyze(actions=['emotion']). Display emotion label + confidence bar on video frame. Test: each student makes 7 expressions, records what the model detects vs. actual expression. Calculate accuracy per emotion.
classes.materials: Python with deepface, opencv-python, numpy; webcam or phone camera via DroidCam; expression accuracy log sheet

classes.title: Build an Emotion Dashboard – Visualise & Reflect
classes.description: Extend the app: log emotion detections to a CSV every 2 seconds. After a 5-minute session (group of 5 students interacting naturally), load CSV into pandas, plot bar chart of emotion frequencies using matplotlib. Discuss: Which emotions were most detected? Was the model fair across different students? Print and display charts.
classes.materials: Completed emotion reader script, pandas, matplotlib, CSV output, A3 printed chart template, ethics reflection sheet

projects.project:
Project 5 – Smart Waste Sorting System
(Colour Sensor + Servo + Classification Logic)

classes.title: Waste Segregation Science – Why It Matters
classes.description: Case study: Bengaluru's waste management crisis vs. Japanese waste sorting system. Video: automated sorting conveyor belts in recycling plants. Students classify 15 sample items (dry, wet, hazardous) on a worksheet. Map the robot's role: detect waste colour/type → sort into correct bin. Define system block diagram.
classes.materials: Projector, waste sorting video, 15 sample item pictures, sorting worksheet, block diagram sheet

classes.title: Wire the Colour Sensor & Servos
classes.description: Wire TCS3200 colour sensor (digital output → Arduino) to read RGB values. Calibrate: place red, green, blue, black, white samples — record sensor outputs. Wire 2 servo motors (one per bin lid). Code: map RGB readings to waste category (dry=high red, wet=high green, metal=silver). Servos open correct bin lid for 2 seconds.
classes.materials: TCS3200 colour sensor, 2× SG90 servos, cardboard tri-bin model, Arduino Uno, coloured waste samples (red paper = hazardous, green paper = wet, white = dry)

classes.title: Test & Improve the Sorting Bot
classes.description: Run 20 trials: place sample items under sensor, observe sorting. Track results in table: item type, detected colour, bin chosen, correct? Calculate sorting accuracy %. Identify failure cases (similar colours), add IF-ELSE logic to resolve conflicts. Bonus: add LCD display showing last sorted item category.
classes.materials: Completed sorting system, 20 sample items, result tracking spreadsheet, LCD I2C module (optional bonus)

projects.project:
Project 6 – Weather Prediction AI
(sklearn Linear Regression + Data Analysis)

classes.title: From Barometer to Machine Learning – Weather Data Science
classes.description: Students get a 365-row CSV dataset (temperature, humidity, pressure, wind_speed, rainfall). Load in Python + pandas. Describe: .shape, .describe(), .info(). Plot scatter plots: humidity vs. rainfall, temperature vs. wind_speed. Identify correlations visually. Define ML task: predict tomorrow's rainfall using today's weather parameters.
classes.materials: Python with pandas, matplotlib, seaborn; 365-row weather_dataset.csv; correlation analysis worksheet

classes.title: Train a Regression Model – Predict Tomorrow's Rain
classes.description: Split data: 80% train, 20% test (train_test_split). Features: temperature, humidity, pressure, wind_speed. Target: rainfall_mm. Train LinearRegression, then try RandomForestRegressor. Evaluate: MAE, RMSE, R². Plot: actual vs. predicted rainfall scatter. Tune: add/remove features, compare RMSE.
classes.materials: Python with sklearn, pandas, matplotlib; weather_dataset.csv; model performance comparison sheet

classes.title: Build a Prediction Interface + Real-World Evaluation
classes.description: Build a terminal-based or Tkinter UI: enter today's weather → get predicted rainfall for tomorrow. Test with 5 days of real local data (students look up yesterday's weather from IMD website). Compare prediction vs. actual. Discuss: Why isn't the model perfect? What data would improve it? Presentation of findings to class.
classes.materials: Completed model, Tkinter or terminal UI code, IMD website access, model evaluation summary sheet

projects.project:
Project 7 – Drone Frame Design
(Fusion 360 + FEA Load Analysis)

classes.title: Drone Aerodynamics & Frame Physics
classes.description: Explain quadcopter mechanics: thrust from 4 motors, frame stiffness vs. weight trade-off. Show Fusion 360 pre-made drone frame: label arms, motor mounts, central hub, frame rails. Students fill a 'drone spec sheet': What shape arm (H-frame vs X-frame)? What material (carbon fibre, ABS, PLA)? Why does arm length affect stability?
classes.materials: Projector, Fusion 360 (pre-installed), drone frame spec sheet, aerodynamics reference card

classes.title: Design the Drone Frame in Fusion 360
classes.description: Students model: central hub (100mm × 100mm × 5mm plate), 4 arms (150mm × 15mm × 5mm extending from hub), motor mounts (30mm diameter circles at arm ends). Use: Sketch → Extrude → Fillet edges (to reduce stress concentrations). Check mass using the physical material (PLA: 1.24 g/cm³). Target: frame mass < 120g.
classes.materials: Fusion 360 (licensed or education version), laptop/desktop, drone frame design checklist, mass calculation sheet

classes.title: FEA Simulation – Can the Frame Handle the Load?
classes.description: Apply Fusion 360 Simulation: fix central hub, apply 500g downward force at each motor mount (simulating takeoff thrust). Run stress analysis. View colour stress map: identify red zones (highest stress). Students modify: add fillet radii at arm-hub junction, rerun simulation. Compare max stress before and after modification. Document findings.
classes.materials: Fusion 360 with Simulation workspace, FEA result screenshot template, stress comparison worksheet

projects.project:
Project 8 – Smart Classroom Automation
(4-Sensor Integration + Flask Dashboard)

classes.title: IoT Architecture for Classrooms – System Design
classes.description: Map out smart classroom requirements: lighting (PIR + LDR), temperature logging (DHT22), air quality (MQ135 CO₂ proxy), attendance (RFID). Draw full system architecture: 4 sensors → Arduino Mega → Serial → Python → SQLite → Flask web dashboard. Students explain each component in their architecture poster.
classes.materials: Projector, architecture worksheet (A3), sticky notes, coloured markers

classes.title: Wire 4 Sensors – Arduino Mega Integration
classes.description: Connect: PIR sensor (motion → lights via relay), LDR (auto-dim lights), DHT22 (temperature + humidity), MQ135 (air quality, returns analog 0–1023). Code: read all 4 sensors in loop, print CSV: motion,lux,temp,humidity,air_quality. Test each sensor independently then combined. Verify serial output on monitor.
classes.materials: Arduino Mega, PIR sensor, LDR + 10kΩ resistor, DHT22 sensor, MQ135 gas sensor, relay module, 5V fan, LED strip, wires, laptop

classes.title: Python + Flask – Build the Live Dashboard
classes.description: Python script reads Arduino serial → stores in SQLite (sensor_log table). Flask app: 5 endpoints serving live charts (Chart.js) for each sensor. Dashboard shows: motion status (on/off), temperature trend (line chart), air quality gauge. Test from another device on same Wi-Fi. Students demo to class.
classes.materials: Python with Flask, sqlite3, pyserial; Chart.js CDN; SQLite DB; second laptop or phone for web view demo

projects.project:
Project 9 – NLP Text Classification Chatbot
(NLTK + TF-IDF + Intent Recognition)

classes.title: Natural Language Processing – How Chatbots Understand Text
classes.description: Demo a rule-based chatbot vs. an NLP chatbot. Explain NLP pipeline: tokenisation → stopword removal → stemming → TF-IDF vectorisation → classifier. Students manually tokenise and stem 5 sentences using a worksheet (Porter stemmer rules). Introduce intent categories: greeting, farewell, question, complaint, request.
classes.materials: Projector, NLP pipeline diagram, tokenisation worksheet (5 sentences per student), Porter stemmer rules card

classes.title: Build & Train the Intent Classifier
classes.description: Dataset: 200 labelled sentences across 5 intent classes (provided CSV). Load with pandas → tokenise + stem with NLTK → TF-IDF vectorise (sklearn). Split 80/20, train MultinomialNB (Naive Bayes) classifier. Evaluate: accuracy, confusion matrix, classification_report. Test with custom sentences typed by students.
classes.materials: Python with NLTK, sklearn, pandas; intents_dataset.csv (200 rows); model evaluation worksheet

classes.title: Build the Chat Interface + Improve the Model
classes.description: Wrap classifier in a chat loop: user types message → preprocess → predict intent → look up response from a response_dict. Add: confidence threshold (if confidence < 0.6, reply 'I didn't understand'). Test: 20 real questions students would ask a school chatbot. Count: how many answered correctly? Suggest 5 training data improvements.
classes.materials: Completed intent classifier, response_dict.json, chat interface script, 20-question test log, improvement suggestion sheet

projects.project:
Project 10 – Grade 8 Capstone
(ML + Hardware + Integrated Presentation)

classes.title: Capstone Brief & Team Design Sprint
classes.description: Groups of 3 choose a capstone problem from 5 options: (A) Automated attendance + emotion check-in, (B) Smart classroom complete, (C) Crop disease detection + watering bot, (D) AI waste sorting + data dashboard, (E) Open innovation. Complete design brief: problem → sensors/components → AI model → output/actuator → success metric.
classes.materials: Capstone brief templates (5 options), group selection sheet, component inventory list

classes.title: Build & Integrate – Hardware + ML Combined
classes.description: Groups build their hardware circuit (using previously learned skills) AND train/integrate an ML model component. Milestone checks at 30 and 40 minutes. Each group must demo: (1) hardware doing something, (2) AI/code component running. Teacher checks: Does the integration between hardware and software work?
classes.materials: Component bins (sensors, actuators, Arduinos, Raspberry Pi if available), laptops with Python, soldering iron (teacher-operated), integration checklist

classes.title: Final Demo Day – Panel Presentation
classes.description: 5-minute demo per group in front of a panel (2 teachers + 1 invited senior student). Structure: (1) Problem & solution overview (1 min), (2) Live hardware demo (2 min), (3) AI/software demo (1 min), (4) Results/metrics (30 sec), (5) What we'd improve (30 sec). Panel scores using rubric. Top 2 projects recorded as short videos.
classes.materials: Completed capstone projects, demo table setup, presentation rubric, video recording device, award certificates

projects.project:
Project 1 – Autonomous Navigation Robot
(IMU + Encoders + PID + Grid Mapping)

classes.title: Localisation & Dead Reckoning – How Robots Know Where They Are
classes.description: Introduce the problem of robot localisation: GPS doesn't work indoors. Teach dead reckoning: using encoder tick counts + known wheel circumference to estimate position. Students manually calculate: if wheel circumference = 21.5cm and encoder counts 100 ticks per revolution, how far has the robot moved for 250 ticks? Introduce IMU: accelerometer for orientation, gyroscope for angular rate. Hands-on: shake MPU6050 module and watch serial output values change.
classes.materials: Projector, MPU6050 IMU module, Arduino Uno, Serial Monitor, dead reckoning calculation worksheet, encoder tick→distance conversion table

classes.title: Wire IMU + Encoders – Sensor Fusion
classes.description: Connect MPU6050 (I2C: SDA→A4, SCL→A5) and wheel encoders (optical: D2, D3 interrupt pins). Code: read encoder counts in interrupt service routines (ISR) — non-blocking. Read IMU yaw angle using complementary filter (95% gyro + 5% accelerometer). Print: left_count, right_count, heading_deg to Serial. Students plot heading while manually pushing robot in a square.
classes.materials: 2WD chassis, MPU6050, 2× optical encoders (slotted disk + IR), Arduino Mega, L298N motor driver, wire, laptop with Serial Plotter

classes.title: Implement PID Heading Control
classes.description: Code a PID controller for heading: error = target_angle − current_angle. P term: proportional correction to motors. I term: accumulated error (handles drift). D term: rate of change (prevents overshoot). Students tune Kp, Ki, Kd by trial — watch robot spin to target heading and stop accurately. Target: reach within ±3° of target heading in <3 seconds.
classes.materials: Completed robot with IMU, PID tuning worksheet (Kp/Ki/Kd log table), Serial Plotter to visualise heading convergence

classes.title: Autonomous Grid Navigation Mission
classes.description: Programme: navigate a 1m × 1m grid (4 waypoints) using encoder-based distance + PID heading control. Mission: start → move 50cm forward → turn right 90° → move 50cm → turn right → return to start. Measure: position error at end vs. start (ideal: < 5cm). Analyse: where does drift accumulate? What would improve accuracy (kalman filter)?
classes.materials: Completed navigation robot, 1m×1m grid mat, measuring tape, navigation error log, Kalman filter teaser worksheet

projects.project:
Project 2 – AI Health Diagnosis System
(Logistic Regression + SHAP Explainability)

classes.title: Machine Learning for Healthcare – Datasets, Features, Ethics
classes.description: Case studies: AI detecting diabetic retinopathy (Google), cancer detection in mammograms (MIT). Students explore the PIMA Indians Diabetes Dataset (768 rows, 8 features, binary outcome). Discussion: What do the features mean? (glucose, BMI, blood pressure). What does it mean if the model is 75% accurate — who does it misclassify? Introduce class imbalance: 65% no diabetes vs. 35% diabetes.
classes.materials: Projector, diabetes_dataset.csv, feature description handout, class imbalance discussion worksheet

classes.title: Train & Evaluate the Classifier
classes.description: Load dataset in pandas → handle missing zeros (replace with median) → scale features (StandardScaler) → split 80/20 → train LogisticRegression, then RandomForestClassifier. Evaluate: accuracy, precision, recall, F1, AUC-ROC. Plot ROC curve. Tune: adjust decision threshold from 0.5 to 0.4 (higher recall). Record: what happens to precision when recall goes up?
classes.materials: Python with sklearn, pandas, matplotlib; diabetes_dataset.csv; model comparison worksheet (5 metrics per model)

classes.title: SHAP Explainability – Why Did the Model Decide This?
classes.description: Install shap library. Generate SHAP values for 10 test predictions. Plot: (1) SHAP beeswarm plot (global feature importance), (2) SHAP waterfall plot for 1 specific prediction. Students explain in plain English: 'This patient was classified as high risk because their glucose level of 180 pushed the score up by +0.42.' Discuss: Is this explanation good enough for a doctor to trust?
classes.materials: Python with shap library, completed diabetes classifier, SHAP explanation worksheet, 10 sample test predictions

classes.title: Clinical Simulation + Ethics Panel
classes.description: Role-play: 3 students are 'patients' (given data cards), 3 are 'doctors' (use model to predict), 1 is 'AI ethicist' (questions every decision). Run 6 predictions, doctors explain model decision using SHAP. Ethicist challenges: 'What if the patient is pregnant — is BMI still valid?' 'The dataset has no Indian patients — is this model valid here?' Written reflection: 3 improvements for real clinical deployment.
classes.materials: Patient data cards (10 sample predictions), doctor decision log, ethics challenge question cards, reflection worksheet

projects.project:
Project 3 – Smart City Simulation
(MQTT + Node-RED + Multi-Sensor Dashboard)

classes.title: IoT Protocols – MQTT Architecture & Publish-Subscribe
classes.description: Explain difference between HTTP (request-response) and MQTT (publish-subscribe). Draw: broker, publisher (sensor), subscriber (dashboard). Set up local Mosquitto MQTT broker on teacher laptop. Students use MQTT Explorer on their laptops to subscribe to test/topic and receive messages. Teach topic naming convention: city/zone1/temperature, city/zone1/traffic.
classes.materials: Mosquitto broker (teacher laptop), MQTT Explorer installed on student laptops, topic structure worksheet, LAN Wi-Fi

classes.title: Wire 3 Sensor Nodes – Publish to MQTT
classes.description: 3 student groups each set up a sensor node: (A) DHT22 + Arduino + ESP8266 → publishes temperature/humidity; (B) IR sensor (vehicle count proxy) → publishes traffic density; (C) LDR → publishes light level (streetlight proxy). Each group publishes to their city/zoneN/sensor topic every 5 seconds. All students see all sensors in MQTT Explorer.
classes.materials: 3× Arduino Uno, 3× ESP8266 Wi-Fi module, DHT22, IR sensor, LDR, 10kΩ resistors, MQTT broker, MQTT Explorer

classes.title: Node-RED Dashboard – Smart City Control Panel
classes.description: Import Node-RED on teacher laptop (or Raspberry Pi). Students design a city dashboard: (1) Temperature gauge (zone 1), (2) Traffic density bar chart (zone 2), (3) Streetlight auto-toggle (if LDR < 300 lux → turn ON relay via MQTT command). Test: when LDR is covered → streetlight relay triggers. Show dashboard on projector.
classes.materials: Node-RED (installed), dashboard nodes, MQTT input/output nodes, relay module for streetlight demo, projector

classes.title: Smart City Policy Report
classes.description: Based on 20 minutes of live data collection, students analyse: What time does traffic peak? When do streetlights turn on? Is the temperature normal for the season? Write a 1-page 'City Management Report' with 3 data-backed recommendations (add fans if temp > 32°C, increase signal time if traffic > 80%). Present to class as city councillors.
classes.materials: 20-minute data log (CSV exported from Node-RED), data analysis worksheet, 1-page report template

projects.project:
Project 4 – Robotic Warehouse Sorter
(Conveyor + Computer Vision + Servo Sorting)

classes.title: Warehouse Automation – From Amazon to Flipkart Fulfilment
classes.description: Video: Amazon Kiva robots, Flipkart automated warehouse. Discuss: What decisions does a sorting robot make? (Read barcode/colour → route item to correct bin). Students design their robot's decision tree: parcel colour → bin assignment. Map system: conveyor belt → camera → Raspberry Pi/laptop → servo gates. Define success metric: sort 10 parcels in < 2 minutes.
classes.materials: Projector, warehouse automation video, system design worksheet, decision tree template

classes.title: Build the Conveyor + Camera System
classes.description: Build a cardboard conveyor belt (DC motor + rubber band drive, 30cm × 10cm track). Mount a USB webcam above the belt at a fixed height. Code: OpenCV captures frame → convert to HSV → detect dominant colour of object on belt → classify: red=bin A, green=bin B, blue=bin C. Test: accuracy of colour detection for 10 coloured blocks.
classes.materials: Cardboard conveyor kit, DC motor, rubber bands, USB webcam, laptop with OpenCV, coloured blocks (30 red/green/blue), HSV calibration worksheet

classes.title: Wire Servo Gates – Automated Sorting
classes.description: Add 2 servo motors as gate mechanisms at bin junctions. Code integration: when OpenCV detects colour → send serial command (A/B/C) to Arduino → Arduino moves corresponding servo 90° to route parcel → servo returns to neutral. Run 10-parcel sorting trial. Record: sorted correctly, jammed, missed. Calculate sorting efficiency %.
classes.materials: 2× SG90 servos, Arduino Nano, serial communication script, gate mechanism (cardboard), 10 coloured parcel models, efficiency tracking sheet

classes.title: Performance Optimisation & Scalability Discussion
classes.description: Analyse bottlenecks: where does the system slow down? (Camera lag, servo speed, belt speed). Students propose improvements: use barcode scanner instead of colour (more reliable), add weight sensor (reject overweight parcels), add second camera. Research: How does Amazon handle 1 million parcels/day? What would it take to scale this to a 10-belt warehouse? Written improvement proposal (1 page).
classes.materials: Performance timing data (from trial 3), benchmarking worksheet, scalability proposal template

projects.project:
Project 5 – Face Recognition Attendance System
(LBPH + SQLite + Flask + Privacy Audit)

classes.title: How Face Recognition Works – From Pixels to Identity
classes.description: Explain face recognition pipeline: detect face (Haar Cascade) → align → extract features (LBP histogram in LBPH, or embedding in FaceNet) → compare to database. Show failure cases: recognition fails under bad lighting, with glasses, for darker skin tones (MIT Media Lab Gender Shades study). Students fill a 'recognition conditions' worksheet: test 5 conditions (good light, side angle, glasses, low light, mask).
classes.materials: Projector, OpenCV installed, webcam, Gender Shades study summary, recognition conditions worksheet

classes.title: Train the LBPH Face Recogniser
classes.description: Each student/group creates their own face dataset: 30 photos (different angles, lighting) using webcam + OpenCV capture script. Train cv2.face.LBPHFaceRecognizer_create() on collected photos. Save model to .yml file. Test: recognise each student in real-time — print name + confidence score. Discuss: what confidence threshold should trigger 'unrecognised' (reject)?
classes.materials: OpenCV-python, numpy, webcam, Python data collection script, LBPH trainer script, confidence threshold worksheet

classes.title: SQLite Attendance Database + Flask Interface
classes.description: Design SQLite schema: students(id, name, roll_no), attendance(id, student_id, timestamp, confidence). Code: recognition loop → on successful match (confidence < threshold) → INSERT attendance record. Flask app: show today's attendance table (sorted by time), flag students marked more than once in 5 minutes (anti-spoofing). Demo live attendance marking.
classes.materials: SQLite3, Flask, completed LBPH model, attendance schema SQL file, Flask attendance UI template

classes.title: Privacy Audit – Ethical Deployment Review
classes.description: Simulate a school privacy committee meeting. Students review their system against 6 criteria: (1) Consent obtained? (2) Data encrypted at rest? (3) Who has access to face data? (4) How long is data retained? (5) What if the system misidentifies a student? (6) Is there a manual override? Students write a 'Privacy Impact Assessment' (1 page) and propose 3 technical safeguards.
classes.materials: Privacy audit checklist, Privacy Impact Assessment template, GDPR/DPDP Act 2023 summary card

projects.project:
Project 6 – Stock Market Prediction
(LSTM Neural Network + yfinance + Time Series)

classes.title: Time Series Data & Stock Market Concepts
classes.description: Explain: stock prices as time series — trends, seasonality, noise. Load Reliance Industries historical data (5 years) using yfinance: yf.download('RELIANCE.NS', period='5y'). Plot closing price. Teach: rolling mean (smoothing), daily returns (percentage change), volume as signal. Students identify: 3 uptrends, 2 downtrends, 1 anomaly in the chart.
classes.materials: Python with yfinance, pandas, matplotlib; stock_analysis_worksheet; internet access for data download

classes.title: Prepare LSTM Training Data + Build Model
classes.description: Normalise prices (MinMaxScaler). Create sequences: X = last 60 days, y = day 61 price. Split 80/20. Build LSTM model (Keras): LSTM(50 units) → Dropout(0.2) → Dense(1). Compile: Adam, MSE loss. Train 20 epochs (show live training log). Plot: training loss vs. validation loss. Detect overfitting.
classes.materials: Python with tensorflow/keras, sklearn, numpy, pandas; pre-downloaded RELIANCE.NS 5-year CSV (fallback if no internet); LSTM architecture diagram worksheet

classes.title: Evaluate & Interpret Predictions
classes.description: Generate predictions on test set. Inverse-transform to actual prices. Plot: actual vs. predicted prices (line chart). Calculate: RMSE, MAPE. Discuss: the model predicts smooth trends but misses sharp drops (why? — LSTMs lag on sudden news events). Students research: 'What caused this spike in Jan 2024?' (actual historical event). Reflect: Can an LSTM predict stock crashes? Why/why not?
classes.materials: Completed LSTM model, prediction plot, RMSE/MAPE calculation sheet, 3 annotated market events from chart

projects.project:
Project 7 – 3D Prototype: Product Design
(Fusion 360 + FEA + Design Review)

classes.title: Product Design Brief & Biomimicry Research
classes.description: Students choose a product to design (options: ergonomic pen grip, foldable phone stand, lightweight bicycle handlebar, noise-reducing earphone case). Research biomimicry: how does nature solve the same problem? (Owl feathers for noise reduction, honeycomb for lightweight strength). Fill a design brief: (1) User, (2) Problem, (3) Constraints (max mass, max dimensions), (4) Biomimicry inspiration.
classes.materials: Projector, biomimicry examples handout, design brief template, product options reference sheet

classes.title: 3D Modelling in Fusion 360 – Full Design
classes.description: Students model their chosen product using: parametric sketching, multi-body modelling (different parts), surface tool for organic shapes, threads for screws. Apply material: ABS, nylon, or aluminium. Render with Fusion 360 Render workspace (studio lighting, chosen colour). Export screenshot: 3 views (front, side, isometric).
classes.materials: Fusion 360 (education licence), laptop, 3-view screenshot template, parametric design checklist

classes.title: FEA + Design Iteration – Strength Check
classes.description: Apply Fusion 360 Simulation: realistic constraints (fixed mounting point, applied force matching real use-case). Run stress analysis. Identify failure point (red zone). Modify: add ribs, change wall thickness, add fillet. Rerun → compare max von Mises stress before/after. Target: reduce max stress by >20% without increasing mass by >15%.
classes.materials: Fusion 360 Simulation workspace, FEA result comparison table, stress-vs-mass optimisation worksheet

classes.title: Design Review Presentation – Client Pitch
classes.description: 5-minute pitch per student/group: (1) Problem + user need, (2) 3D model walkthrough (on screen), (3) FEA stress results, (4) Manufacturing plan (3D printing, injection moulding?), (5) Cost estimate (material + time). Panel: 2 teachers score using engineering design rubric. Top 3 designs selected for actual 3D printing (if printer available).
classes.materials: Rendered 3D model, FEA results summary, pitch slide deck (3 slides), engineering design rubric, cost estimation worksheet

projects.project:
Project 8 – IoT Smart Electricity Meter
(PZEM-004T + InfluxDB + WhatsApp Alert)

classes.title: Smart Metering Technology – How DISCOM Reads Your Meter
classes.description: Compare: traditional meter reading (manual, monthly) vs. smart meter (real-time, two-way communication). Explain PZEM-004T module: measures voltage, current, power, frequency, energy (kWh). Wire PZEM-004T to a safe low-power load (USB charger, 5W LED) through clamp. Read values via Arduino Modbus RTU. Print live: Voltage: 229.8V, Current: 0.021A, Power: 4.8W.
classes.materials: PZEM-004T module, Arduino Mega, RS485 module, 5W LED load, clamp-style wiring, Serial Monitor, live reading log sheet

classes.title: InfluxDB Time-Series Storage + Grafana Dashboard
classes.description: Set up InfluxDB (local or InfluxDB Cloud free tier). Python script: read PZEM data via pyserial → write to InfluxDB (measurement: power_reading, tags: device_id, fields: voltage, current, power, energy). Create Grafana dashboard: (1) Real-time watt gauge, (2) Energy kWh accumulator, (3) 1-hour power trend chart. Connect Grafana to InfluxDB datasource.
classes.materials: Python with influxdb-client, pyserial; InfluxDB (local or cloud); Grafana (local); Grafana setup guide

classes.title: WhatsApp Alert + Cost Calculator
classes.description: Integrate Twilio API (or WhatsApp Business API test mode): if power > 500W for > 30 seconds → send WhatsApp message to registered number: 'Alert: High power draw detected – 620W. Please check connected appliances.' Code daily cost calculator: energy_kWh × 7.5 (₹/kWh) = daily cost. Display on Grafana panel.
classes.materials: Twilio API credentials, Python with twilio library, cost calculation logic, Grafana + WhatsApp integration test worksheet

classes.title: Field Study – Classroom Energy Audit
classes.description: Deploy the meter on the classroom's main power strip for 45 minutes. Students record readings every 5 minutes. Calculate: total energy used, projected monthly cost, CO₂ equivalent (using India grid emission factor: 0.82 kg CO₂/kWh). Write a 1-page 'Energy Audit Report' with 3 recommendations to reduce consumption (e.g. switch to LEDs, turn off AC 30 min early).
classes.materials: Deployed smart meter, 45-minute data log, energy audit report template, India CO₂ emission factor reference, InfluxDB export

projects.project:
Project 9 – Disaster Response Robot
(Terrain Navigation + Camera + Thermal Sensor)

classes.title: Disaster Robotics – Real Deployments & Design Requirements
classes.description: Case studies: Boston Dynamics Spot in disaster zones, DARPA Robotics Challenge, India NDRF remote sensing drones. Discuss requirements: What must a disaster robot do? (navigate rubble, detect heat signatures, transmit video, survive dust/water). Students fill a 'requirements matrix': must have / nice to have / not required. Map system architecture: robot → camera feed → thermal sensor → base station display.
classes.materials: Projector, disaster robotics case studies, requirements matrix worksheet, system architecture template

classes.title: Build the Terrain Robot – All-Terrain Chassis
classes.description: Assemble a 4-wheel drive chassis (or track-based chassis) — higher ground clearance than 2WD. Connect 2× L298N motor drivers (for 4 motors). Wire ultrasonic sensor (front obstacle). Wire Pi Camera or USB webcam. Test: navigate a rubble simulation (foam blocks, inclined cardboard). Code: tank steering (left motors/right motors independently).
classes.materials: 4WD all-terrain chassis kit, 2× L298N motor drivers, Arduino Mega, HC-SR04, USB webcam or Pi Camera, inclined cardboard ramp, foam obstacle blocks

classes.title: Add Thermal Sensor + Video Streaming
classes.description: Connect MLX90614 non-contact IR thermal sensor (I2C). Code: read object_temperature — if > 37°C (human body proxy) → flash red LED + beep buzzer (victim detected signal). For video: use OpenCV on a connected laptop (receiving webcam serial stream) or Raspberry Pi. Students pilot robot through obstacle course, find 'victim' (heated object), mark location.
classes.materials: MLX90614 thermal sensor, red LED, buzzer, completed 4WD robot, Raspberry Pi or laptop, OpenCV video display script, heated cup as 'victim'

classes.title: Mission Debrief + Improvement Proposal
classes.description: Run 3 timed rescue missions: find 1 heated object in a 2m × 2m obstacle field. Record: time to find, false alarms, obstacles hit. Calculate: mission success rate. Students write 'Mission Debrief Report': (1) System performance, (2) Failure modes (thermal sensor false positive in sunny room), (3) 3 design improvements (add GPS, add 360° camera, add arm gripper). Present findings.
classes.materials: 3-mission result data sheet, debrief report template, improvement sketch sheet

projects.project:
Project 10 – Grade 9 Research & Innovation
(Academic Paper + Symposium)

classes.title: Research Methodology – From Observation to Hypothesis
classes.description: Teach: scientific method applied to engineering projects. Examine 3 published school/undergraduate research papers (simplified). Identify structure: abstract, introduction, methodology, results, discussion, conclusion. Students select their research topic from completed Grade 9 projects (or propose a new one). Write: problem statement, hypothesis, methodology outline (1 page).
classes.materials: 3 simplified research paper PDFs, research paper structure template, topic selection worksheet, problem statement guide

classes.title: Conduct Experiments – Collect & Analyse Data
classes.description: Students run structured experiments for their chosen topic: minimum 3 trials per condition, controlled variables documented. Record data in tables. Analyse: mean, standard deviation, statistical significance (basic). Plot: bar chart or line graph of results. Compare to hypothesis: was the prediction correct? Identify sources of error.
classes.materials: Experiment materials (from their chosen project), data collection table template, statistical analysis worksheet, graphing software (Excel or Python)

classes.title: Write the Research Paper
classes.description: Using IEEE conference paper template (simplified): write all 6 sections. Abstract: 150 words max. Introduction: context + problem. Methodology: describe experiment. Results: tables + figures (reference numbered). Discussion: what do results mean? Conclusion: what was proven + future work. Peer review: swap papers with another group, give written feedback using a rubric.
classes.materials: IEEE paper template (simplified A4), peer review rubric, writing quality checklist, word processor or Google Docs

classes.title: Research Symposium – Public Presentation
classes.description: Students present at the Grade 9 Research Symposium: 7-minute presentation + 3-minute Q&A. Panel: 2 teachers + Grade 10 mentors. Evaluation criteria: (1) Research quality, (2) Data validity, (3) Clarity of presentation, (4) Handling of questions, (5) Practical significance. Top 2 papers submitted to an inter-school STEM fair if available.
classes.materials: Printed research papers, presentation slides (max 8 slides), symposium evaluation rubric, award certificates

projects.project:
Project 1 – Self-Driving Car Simulation
(Lane Detection + OpenCV + Hardware Integration)

classes.title: Autonomous Vehicle Technology – Layers of Self-Driving
classes.description: Explain SAE levels 0–5 of autonomy. Anatomy of a self-driving car: sensors (LiDAR, radar, camera), perception (object detection, lane finding), planning (path planning), control (steering/throttle). Focus for this project: camera-only lane detection (Level 2 – lane assist). Watch Tesla FSD footage and identify what the system is 'seeing'. Students map the software pipeline for their scale-model car.
classes.materials: Projector, SAE levels handout, AV software pipeline worksheet, Tesla FSD video clip

classes.title: Lane Detection Algorithm – OpenCV Hough Transform
classes.description: On a laptop: load a road image → convert to grayscale → Gaussian blur → Canny edge detection → region of interest mask → HoughLinesP to detect lane lines → draw green lane overlay. Students tune: Canny thresholds, Hough parameters. Test on 5 different road images (straight, curve, night, rain, highway). Measure: false detections per image.
classes.materials: Python with OpenCV, numpy; 5 road test images; lane detection parameter tuning worksheet

classes.title: Hardware Car + Lane Following Controller
classes.description: Build/extend a 2WD car with a front-facing USB webcam. Run lane detection on a laptop receiving camera stream. Compute lane centre offset → steering correction (if offset left → turn right). Serial command to Arduino for motor steering. Test on a tape-road track (3m straight + 2 curves). Measure: how many seconds can it stay in lane before drifting?
classes.materials: 2WD car chassis, USB webcam, Arduino Nano, L298N, laptop with Python + OpenCV, tape road track, lane-staying time log

classes.title: Simulation Stress Test + AV Ethics Discussion
classes.description: Test car in 3 challenging scenarios: (A) Occlusion (hand covers 1 lane line), (B) Glare (torch on track), (C) Merged lanes (tape joined). Record failure modes. Introduce AV ethics: trolley problem for AVs, liability in accidents, bias in training data. Students write a 2-paragraph 'Ethics of Autonomous Vehicles' position paper and present to class.
classes.materials: Stress test scenarios setup, failure mode log sheet, AV ethics position paper template, trolley problem scenario cards

projects.project:
Project 2 – AI Recommendation System
(Collaborative Filtering + Flask + A/B Test)

classes.title: Recommendation Engine Science – Netflix, Amazon, Spotify
classes.description: Explain how recommendations work: content-based (item features), collaborative filtering (user behaviour matrix), hybrid. Show the user-item matrix concept: rows = users, columns = movies, values = ratings (1–5). Demonstrate matrix sparsity: 99% of cells are empty. Introduce cosine similarity for finding similar users. Students manually compute cosine similarity between 2 users given a 5-item rating matrix.
classes.materials: Projector, user-item matrix worksheet (manual cosine similarity calculation), Netflix recommendation case study handout

classes.title: Build Collaborative Filtering in Python
classes.description: Use MovieLens 100K dataset (or school-constructed book rating dataset). Load with pandas, pivot to user-item matrix. Compute user-user cosine similarity (sklearn). For a target user: find top 5 similar users → aggregate their unseen ratings → recommend top 5 items. Evaluate: split data 80/20, calculate precision@5 (how many recommendations were actually liked?).
classes.materials: Python with pandas, sklearn, numpy; movielens_100k.csv or school_book_ratings.csv; recommendation evaluation worksheet

classes.title: Flask Web App – Personalised Recommendation Interface
classes.description: Build a Flask web app: user enters their name → app loads their profile from CSV → generates top 5 recommendations → displays with item name, predicted rating, explanation ('Users similar to you also liked...'). Add: user feedback button (thumbs up/down) → log to feedback.csv. Run in class: each student uses the app and rates 3 recommendations.
classes.materials: Flask, pandas, completed CF model, HTML recommendation UI template, feedback.csv logging

classes.title: A/B Test – Does Personalisation Work?
classes.description: Design an A/B test: Group A (10 students) gets personalised recommendations; Group B (10 students) gets popularity-based recommendations (most-rated items). Both groups rate whether they'd read/watch each recommendation (1–5). Analyse: average satisfaction score (A vs. B), statistical significance (t-test). Report: did personalisation significantly outperform popularity? What does this mean for real products?
classes.materials: A/B test data collection sheet, t-test worksheet (manual or scipy.stats), A/B test analysis report template

projects.project:
Project 3 – Smart Surveillance System
(YOLO Object Detection + DeepSORT Tracking + Ethics Audit)

classes.title: Computer Vision in Surveillance – Technical Architecture
classes.description: Show a CCTV analytics system (commercial demo). Explain pipeline: video frames → YOLO object detection (bounding boxes, labels, confidence) → DeepSORT multi-object tracking (assigns persistent IDs across frames) → alert trigger (if person in restricted zone). Students draw their surveillance system architecture and define: what objects to detect, what events trigger alerts, who sees the alerts.
classes.materials: Projector, YOLO detection demo (pre-run video), DeepSORT tracking video, system architecture worksheet

classes.title: Run YOLO + DeepSORT – Live Detection & Tracking
classes.description: Use YOLOv8 (ultralytics, pre-installed). Load webcam stream: model.predict(source=0, stream=True). Draw bounding boxes, class labels, confidence scores. Integrate DeepSORT: each detected person gets a unique Track ID. Log: track_id, class, confidence, timestamp to CSV. Test: 3 people walk in front of camera — IDs should persist even when person turns around.
classes.materials: Python with ultralytics (YOLOv8), deep-sort-realtime; webcam; laptop with GPU or pre-processed video file; tracking accuracy log

classes.title: Alert System + Zone Detection
classes.description: Define a restricted zone (draw a polygon on the video frame using mouse-click coordinate capture). Code: if any detected person's bounding box centroid falls inside the polygon → log alert → flash red border on video + send email alert (smtplib). Test: walk into and out of zone 10 times. Count: true alerts, missed alerts, false alerts.
classes.materials: Zone polygon definition script, smtplib email alert code, alert accuracy test worksheet, restricted zone floor markings

classes.title: Ethics Audit – Responsible Deployment Review
classes.description: Students conduct a formal ethics audit of their system using the EU AI Act High-Risk AI checklist (simplified): (1) Is facial recognition involved? (2) Is consent displayed? (3) Is data encrypted? (4) Can individuals request deletion? (5) Is there human oversight of alerts? (6) Is the system accessible to people with disabilities? Students score their system (0–2 per criterion, max 12) and write a 'Responsible AI Deployment Certificate' noting what must be fixed before real deployment.
classes.materials: EU AI Act checklist (simplified), ethics scoring rubric, Responsible AI Deployment Certificate template

projects.project:
Project 4 – Crop Yield Prediction AI
(Streamlit + geopandas + Choropleth Map)

classes.title: Agricultural AI in India – Data Sources & Problem Framing
classes.description: Introduce: Fasal, Cropin, ICRISAT AI for crops. Dataset: India State-wise Crop Yield (Kaggle: 15,000 rows, features: state, crop, season, area, production, rainfall, fertilizer). Explore in pandas: which state has highest wheat yield? Which season dominates rice? Plot: top 10 crops by area (bar chart). Define ML task: given state, crop, season, area, rainfall, fertilizer → predict yield (tonnes/hectare).
classes.materials: Python with pandas, matplotlib, seaborn; india_crop_yield.csv (Kaggle dataset); exploratory analysis worksheet

classes.title: Train Yield Prediction Model + Feature Engineering
classes.description: Feature engineering: encode categorical (state, crop, season) using LabelEncoder. Train RandomForestRegressor. Evaluate: RMSE, R². Feature importance chart: which factors most affect yield? Tune: add/remove 'rainfall' feature — does it help? Cross-validate (5-fold). Students compare: RF vs. GradientBoosting vs. XGBoost (if installed). Report: best model and why.
classes.materials: Python with sklearn, xgboost (optional), pandas; feature engineering worksheet; model comparison table

classes.title: Streamlit App – Interactive Yield Prediction Tool
classes.description: Build a Streamlit app: dropdowns for state, crop, season; sliders for area (hectares), rainfall (mm), fertilizer (kg/ha). On button click: run model prediction → display predicted yield in large font + comparison to state average. Run locally: streamlit run app.py. Demo in class: students enter parameters for their home state and crop.
classes.materials: Python with Streamlit, completed RF model (saved as .pkl), app.py template, streamlit run instructions

classes.title: Choropleth Map – Visualise Yield Across India
classes.description: Load India state shapefile (geopandas). Merge with state-level average yield predictions from the model. Plot: choropleth map (colour = yield level) for wheat, season = Rabi. Add: tooltip showing state name + yield on hover (using folium). Export as HTML map. Students identify: 3 low-yield states → propose interventions (irrigation, better seeds, fertilizer subsidy).
classes.materials: Python with geopandas, folium, matplotlib; india_states_shapefile; choropleth map worksheet; policy intervention template

projects.project:
Project 5 – Industrial Robotic Arm
(6-DOF Kinematics + Metal Geared Servos)

classes.title: Robotic Arm Mechanics – Degrees of Freedom & Workspace
classes.description: Explain DOF: each joint adds 1 degree of freedom. Draw: 1-DOF (shoulder), 2-DOF (shoulder+elbow), 6-DOF (full industrial arm). Forward kinematics: given joint angles → find end-effector position. Inverse kinematics: given target position → find joint angles (harder!). Students manually calculate FK for a 2-DOF arm (given: L1=10cm, L2=8cm, θ1=30°, θ2=45° → find X,Y). Introduce DH parameters.
classes.materials: Projector, robotic arm kinematics worksheet (FK manual calculation), DH parameter table reference, real industrial arm video

classes.title: Build the 6-DOF Arm – Metal Gear Servos
classes.description: Assemble a 6-DOF arm kit (metal-geared MG996R servos for base, shoulder, elbow; SG90 for wrist, wrist-rotate, gripper). Attach arm to base mount. Connect all 6 servos to PCA9685 16-channel PWM servo driver (I2C to Arduino/Raspberry Pi). Test: move each servo individually to verify range of motion. Check: no servo binding or backlash issues.
classes.materials: 6-DOF arm kit (metal + plastic parts), 3× MG996R servos, 3× SG90 servos, PCA9685 PWM board, 5V 3A power supply, hex keys, Arduino Mega

classes.title: Python IK Controller – Point-to-Point Motion
classes.description: Install ikpy library. Define arm model in ikpy (link lengths + joint limits from the physical arm). Code: input target X,Y,Z → ikpy.chain.inverse_kinematics() → joint angles → send to PCA9685 → arm moves to position. Test: 5 target positions (place a marker on table). Measure: position error (target vs. actual end-effector position using a ruler).
classes.materials: Python with ikpy, adafruit-circuitpython-pca9685; completed 6-DOF arm; position accuracy log; ruler for measurement

classes.title: Pick & Place Automation – Programmed Task
classes.description: Programme an automated pick-and-place sequence: arm moves to pick position → gripper closes (pick object) → arm moves to place position → gripper opens (place). Object: a small 2cm foam cube. Run 10 pick-and-place cycles. Measure: success rate, cycle time. Improve: add a webcam for visual feedback (detect cube with OpenCV blob detection before gripping).
classes.materials: Completed 6-DOF arm, 5× 2cm foam cubes, webcam (optional), pick-and-place sequence script, 10-cycle success rate log

projects.project:
Project 6 – AI Resume Screener
(BERT Embeddings + Bias Audit + De-biasing)

classes.title: NLP for HR – How AI Reads Resumes
classes.description: Case study: Amazon's scrapped AI hiring tool (biased against women). Explain how text-based AI screening works: parse resume → extract features (skills, education, experience) → score against job description → rank candidates. Introduce BERT: bidirectional transformer that understands context (not just keywords). Demo: sentence_transformers computing similarity between job description and 2 resumes. Students manually rank 5 resume excerpts against a job description.
classes.materials: Projector, Amazon AI hiring case study PDF, 5 resume excerpts, manual ranking worksheet, BERT intro slide

classes.title: Build the Screener – BERT Sentence Similarity
classes.description: Load sentence-transformers model (all-MiniLM-L6-v2). For each resume in a set of 15 (provided CSV with resume_text, candidate_name, gender, ethnicity): compute cosine similarity to job_description_text. Rank resumes by score. Display top 5. Evaluate: does the system produce a coherent ranking? Are high scorers actually more qualified based on manual review?
classes.materials: Python with sentence-transformers, sklearn, pandas; resumes_dataset.csv (15 rows with gender/ethnicity tags); job_description.txt; ranking output sheet

classes.title: Bias Audit – Does the Screener Discriminate?
classes.description: Analyse screening results by demographic: average score for male vs. female candidates; average score for Indian-origin vs. non-Indian names. Use counterfactual testing: take 1 resume, swap only the name (male→female name) — does the score change? (It shouldn't.) Visualise: bar chart of average scores by gender. If bias is found, document: which feature caused it (name, pronoun in text, university).
classes.materials: Bias audit analysis script, counterfactual test worksheet, demographic score comparison chart, bias root cause analysis template

classes.title: De-biasing Techniques + Ethical Deployment Policy
classes.description: Implement 2 de-biasing strategies: (1) Text pre-processing: replace gendered pronouns with neutral (he/she → they), redact names before scoring. (2) Score re-calibration: equalise average scores across demographic groups (post-processing fairness). Re-run audit — does bias reduce? Write a 1-page 'Ethical AI Deployment Policy' for the screener: who audits it, how often, what triggers a human review.
classes.materials: De-biasing code additions, re-run bias audit comparison, Ethical AI Policy template

projects.project:
Project 7 – 3D Startup Product Design
(Fusion 360 + Render + Dragon's Den Pitch)

classes.title: Design Thinking Bootcamp – Empathise to Prototype
classes.description: Run a 40-minute design thinking sprint: Empathise (interview 2 classmates about a daily problem), Define (write problem statement: 'A [user] needs a way to [need] because [insight]'), Ideate (10 ideas in 10 minutes), Prototype concept (thumbnail sketch), Test (show sketch to a classmate — 1 piece of feedback). Choose the best idea for 3D product design.
classes.materials: Design thinking sprint worksheet (5 stages), interview guide, sketch template, feedback card

classes.title: Model the Product in Fusion 360 – Final Build
classes.description: Students model their product in Fusion 360 with full detail: multiple bodies, fillets, chamfers, threaded fasteners, brand engraving (text emboss). Apply appearance: custom colour + material texture (matte plastic, brushed metal). Set up a Render: studio background, 3-point lighting, HDRI environment. Render at medium quality → export image.
classes.materials: Fusion 360 (education), laptop, product modelling checklist, rendered image export guide

classes.title: Business Model Canvas + Market Sizing
classes.description: Fill a Business Model Canvas: Value Proposition, Customer Segments, Revenue Streams, Cost Structure, Key Partners. Calculate: TAM (Total Addressable Market), SAM, SOM using India market data. If product targets school students: TAM = 250M students in India, SAM = urban private school segment (10M), SOM = target 0.1% in year 1 (10,000 units). Revenue: 10,000 × ₹299 = ₹29.9 lakh year 1.
classes.materials: Business Model Canvas template, TAM/SAM/SOM calculation worksheet, India demographic data reference card

classes.title: Dragon's Den Pitch – Live Investment Panel
classes.description: 3-minute pitch + 2-minute Q&A per group. Panel of 3 teachers plays 'Dragons' with ₹10 lakh virtual investment each. Pitch must include: (1) Problem hook, (2) Product demo (rendered 3D on screen), (3) Target market, (4) Business model + revenue, (5) Investment ask + equity offer. Panel grills: 'What if Amazon copies this?' 'What's your moat?' Top pitch wins symbolic investment offer.
classes.materials: Rendered product images, Business Model Canvas summary slide, pitch evaluation rubric, virtual investment tokens

projects.project:
Project 8 – IoT Smart Hospital
(Multi-Sensor + Anomaly Detection + Alert Simulation)

classes.title: Healthcare IoT Architecture – ICU to Cloud
classes.description: Explain hospital IoT stack: bedside sensors → hospital gateway → cloud (FHIR/HL7) → doctor's app → alert. Review parameters: ECG (heart), SpO₂ (oxygen), blood pressure, temperature, respiratory rate. For this project: simulate with Arduino sensors (pulse sensor for HR, thermistor for temperature, potentiometer for SpO₂ proxy). Students design their 'virtual patient monitor' system architecture.
classes.materials: Projector, hospital IoT architecture diagram, patient monitor parameter reference card, system design worksheet

classes.title: Build the Patient Monitor – Multi-Sensor Arduino
classes.description: Wire: MAX30100 pulse oximeter (I2C) for heart rate + SpO₂, DS18B20 waterproof temperature sensor, push-button to simulate BP measurement. Code: read all sensors every 2 seconds, display on 16×2 I2C LCD: HR: 72 bpm | SpO₂: 98% | Temp: 36.8°C. Serial output for Python logging. Test: measure own pulse + SpO₂, compare to phone health app.
classes.materials: MAX30100 module, DS18B20 sensor, 16×2 I2C LCD, Arduino Mega, wires, phone health app for comparison, vital signs accuracy worksheet

classes.title: Anomaly Detection – Python Isolation Forest
classes.description: Log 20 minutes of sensor data to CSV. Load in Python. Train IsolationForest (sklearn) on the normal range data. Test: manually inject anomalies (cover pulse sensor → HR=0, hold breath → SpO₂ drops). Check: does the model flag these as anomalies? Visualise: scatter plot with normal (blue) vs. anomaly (red) points. Set threshold: if anomaly_score > 0.7 → alert.
classes.materials: Python with sklearn (IsolationForest), pandas, matplotlib; 20-min sensor CSV log; anomaly test protocol; anomaly scatter plot worksheet

classes.title: Alert System + Hospital Dashboard Simulation
classes.description: Build a Tkinter or Streamlit dashboard: live vital signs display, anomaly alert banner (red flash), patient history chart (last 10 minutes). Code: if anomaly detected → play alarm sound (pygame) + log alert to SQLite with timestamp + vital values. Simulate a 5-minute 'patient deterioration' scenario. Demo to class: who notices the alert first?
classes.materials: Tkinter or Streamlit, pygame (for alarm sound), SQLite, completed patient monitor, 5-minute deterioration simulation script, demo evaluation sheet

projects.project:
Project 9 – Cybersecurity Lab
(SQL Injection + Wireshark + CTF Challenge)

classes.title: Cybersecurity Foundations – Threat Landscape & Attack Vectors
classes.description: Case studies: India's AIIMS hospital ransomware attack (2022), Aadhaar data breach, SolarWinds supply chain attack. Explain CIA triad (Confidentiality, Integrity, Availability). OWASP Top 10 overview — focus on SQL Injection (#3) and Broken Access Control (#1). Students classify 10 attack scenarios using the CIA triad. Discuss: ethical hacking vs. criminal hacking — what makes the difference legally?
