classes.materials: Projector, AIIMS ransomware case study, OWASP Top 10 reference card, CIA triad classification worksheet

classes.title: SQL Injection – Attack & Defend
classes.description: Set up a deliberately vulnerable web app (DVWA or a custom Flask app with a login form). Students perform SQL injection: enter ' OR '1'='1 in username field → system logs in without a password. Try: ' OR 1=1 -- (comment out password check). Document: what data can be accessed. Then: fix the vulnerability using parameterised queries (Python sqlite3 with ? placeholder). Retest — confirm the fix works.
classes.materials: DVWA or custom vulnerable Flask app (pre-configured), SQLite database, attack test log, parameterised query fix guide

classes.title: Wireshark – Network Traffic Analysis
classes.description: Run Wireshark on school lab network (or a sandboxed local network). Capture HTTP traffic while browsing a non-HTTPS test page. Students filter: http, dns, tcp.port==80. Identify: can you see login credentials sent over HTTP? (Yes — demonstrate danger of HTTP). Show: same login over HTTPS → encrypted, unreadable. Students document 3 protocols, their port numbers, and whether they're encrypted.
classes.materials: Wireshark (installed on lab laptops), non-HTTPS test page (local Flask server), protocol analysis worksheet, HTTPS vs. HTTP comparison

classes.title: CTF Challenge – Capture The Flag
classes.description: Students attempt a 3-challenge mini CTF (Capture The Flag) competition: (1) Find the hidden flag in an HTML page source (inspect element), (2) Crack a weak MD5 hash using an online cracker (hash: student-id → MD5 → crack), (3) Decode a Base64-encoded secret message. First 3 students to submit all flags win. Debrief: map each challenge to real attack types. Write: 'What I learned about cybersecurity' reflection.
classes.materials: CTF challenge sheet (3 puzzles + flag submission form), MD5 hash value cards, Base64 encoded message, flag submission box, reflection worksheet

projects.project:
Project 10 – Grade 10 Final Capstone
(Open House + IEEE Paper + GitHub + Portfolio)

classes.title: Capstone Brief, Team Formation & Project Selection
classes.description: Groups of 2–3 choose a capstone challenge: must integrate AI/ML + hardware OR full-stack software + real data + social impact. Shortlist from 8 options provided, or propose original idea (teacher approval required). Complete a full 1-page Project Specification: problem statement, system architecture diagram, ML/AI component, hardware component (if any), success metrics (quantitative), timeline (3 classes), stakeholder (who benefits).
classes.materials: Capstone project options brief (8 options), project specification template, architecture diagram guide, success metric definition worksheet

classes.title: Build Week – Full System Integration
classes.description: Groups build their capstone over 45 minutes. Mandatory milestones at 15 min (architecture confirmed, code started), 30 min (core functionality working), 45 min (integration tested). Teacher and senior mentors rotate and give 2-minute feedback per group. Every group must commit their code to GitHub by end of class (README.md with project description, installation instructions, usage guide).
classes.materials: GitHub accounts (pre-created), laptops with all required libraries, component bin, teacher + senior mentor feedback cards, GitHub commit checklist

classes.title: IEEE Paper Writing – Abstract to Conclusion
classes.description: Using a simplified IEEE conference paper template (A4, 2-column format): write all sections. Abstract (150 words): summarise problem, method, result. Introduction: context, gap, contribution. System Design/Methodology: explain architecture + ML/hardware components with a diagram. Results: quantitative performance metrics with tables/graphs. Conclusion: summary + 2 future improvements. Each group prints 1 copy for peer review (next class).
classes.materials: IEEE paper template (simplified 2-column A4), paper writing guide, quantitative results table template, peer review rubric

classes.title: Open House – Capstone Exhibition & Portfolio
classes.description: Public exhibition: students demo their projects to an audience (parents, teachers, junior grades, guests). Each station has: live demo, printed poster (A2), project QR code linking to GitHub repo. Students also submit their digital Portfolio: PDF combining all 10 Grade 10 project summaries + reflections + certificates. Panel evaluates: technical depth, presentation quality, social impact, GitHub completeness. Top 3 projects receive Gurukulam Innovation Award.
classes.materials: Exhibition tables, A2 printed posters, QR codes to GitHub repos, portfolio PDF (submitted digitally), evaluation rubric, Gurukulam Innovation Award certificates
